title: 10 best herbal capsules for bloating

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with bloating because they contain a natural compound called anethole, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This inflammation can lead to increased gas and water retention, causing discomfort and bloating. By reducing inflammation, fennel capsules help to alleviate these symptoms, promoting a sense of relief and comfort. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties also help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for easier passage of gas and reducing the likelihood of bloating.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel's essential oils, such as anethole, help to break down gas bubbles in the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort.

Digestive enzyme stimulant properties: Fennel seeds contain enzymes that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which helps to break down food more efficiently, reducing symptoms of bloating and indigestion.

Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's active compounds, such as anethole and limonene, help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to bloating and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Acts as a carminative agent, helping to relieve bloating by reducing gas and discomfort in the digestive tract.
Foeniculin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate bloating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system.

Chalcones (specifically, artocarpin and isofraxidin): Possess anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help ease bloating by reducing muscle cramps and inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and alleviate bloating symptoms.
Leaves: They are used because of their high content of volatile oils, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues.
Stems: They are used due to their rich content of volatile oils and flavonoids, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare seeds from mature plants with 1/4 inch of stem attached during the summer months.
Dry the harvested seeds in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 105 degrees for 2 hours.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 20,000 rpm for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of water to form a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared paste using a capsule filler machine with 500 capsules per hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with bloating because of their ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a contributing factor to bloating. Additionally, licorice capsules can help to regulate bowel movements and improve digestion, allowing for more efficient absorption of nutrients and reducing symptoms of bloating and discomfort. This natural remedy can provide fast and effective relief from uncomfortable bloating.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract that may be contributing to bloating and discomfort.
Licoricidin: This bioactive compound has antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the growth of bad bacteria in the gut that may be causing gas and bloating.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has antioxidant effects, which can help protect the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules help reduce bloating by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down carbohydrates, thereby reducing gas production in the gut.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, helps alleviate bloating by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting the relaxation of smooth muscle, which can contribute to discomfort and bloating.

Liquiritigenin: Liquiritigenin, a flavonoid isomer, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate bloating by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to discomfort and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate bloating symptoms.
Leaves: Although not as commonly used as the roots, some products may include leaves, which contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin, contributing to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra may also be used, but less frequently, to extract glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help to soothe digestive issues and reduce bloating.
Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate bloating symptoms.
Leaves: Although not as commonly used as the roots, some products may include leaves, which contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin, contributing to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500 grams of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powder with 100 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of water to create a uniform mixture.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 2000 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture weighing approximately 150 milligrams each.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years before expiration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsules helps with bloating because they contain carvone, a natural oil that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The antispasmodic properties of carvone help to soothe and relax the muscles in the digestive system, alleviating cramps and discomfort associated with bloating. Additionally, caraway's ability to stimulate digestion can help to break down food more efficiently, reducing the likelihood of gas buildup and subsequent bloating.
remedy_properties: Carumoside: Caraway seeds contain a compound called carumoside, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive cramps associated with bloating.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in caraway seeds, particularly carvone and limonene, have natural digestive properties that can help stimulate the digestion of food, reduce gas production, and alleviate symptoms of bloating and discomfort.

Fiber content: Caraway seeds are a rich source of dietary fiber, which can help regulate bowel movements, soften stool, and prevent constipation, all of which can contribute to reduced bloating and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Carvone: Helps with bloating as it has carminative properties, which aid in relieving gas and indigestion by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.
Ligustilide: Contributes to reducing bloating by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can alleviate digestive discomfort and relieve cramps.
Beta-sitosterol: Aids in reducing bloating by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can soothe digestive tract irritation and alleviate gas and bloating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used due to their carvone and limonene content, which helps alleviate bloating and digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: Caraway rhyzomes (or roots) contain compounds like sesquiterpenes, which may help soothe digestive discomfort and reduce bloating.
Seeds: (Repeated for clarity) Caraway seeds are also used to make capsules for bloating due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties and ability to ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Grind 200 grams of dried carum carvi root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 10 grams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Add 30 milliliters of water to the mixture and stir until it forms a uniform paste using a spoon for 2 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill the mixture into 50-100 empty capsules for 5 minutes.
Store the herbal carum carvi capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with bloating because they contain the herb Angelica archangelica, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. The capsules help to ease digestive discomfort, reduce inflammation in the intestines, and promote the healthy balance of gut bacteria. As a result, herbal angelica capsules can effectively alleviate symptoms of bloating, such as abdominal pain, gas, and discomfort, providing relief for individuals who suffer from digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Angelica's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to bloating.
Carminative property: The carminative properties of angelica capsules help to eliminate gas and reduce pressure in the digestive system, alleviating bloating and discomfort.
Antioxidant property: Angelica's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to digestive issues like bloating and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help to reduce bloating by decreasing inflammation and improving gut motility.
Terpenoids (specifically, sesquiterpenes): They exhibit anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help to alleviate bloating and discomfort.
Apiol: It has carminative properties, helping to reduce gas and alleviate bloating by increasing the movement of food through the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that help relieve bloating.

Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich content of essential oils, particularly angelic acid and isopulegol, which have anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe digestive discomfort.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of volatile oils, which have carminative and anti-inflammatory properties that help ease bloating and digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested angelica archangelica in a single layer on a paper bag in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Crush 30 grams of dried angelica archangelica into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 30 grams of gelatin in a bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes to hydrate.
Fill gelatin capsules with the angelica archangelica mixture using a small spoon and store them in a cool place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with bloating because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the digestive tract. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, also have a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating. Additionally, ginger has been shown to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, helping to eliminate gas and alleviate symptoms of bloating.
remedy_properties: Digestive enzyme stimulant: Herbal ginger capsules stimulate digestive enzymes, which helps to break down food more efficiently and alleviate symptoms of bloating. This property helps to reduce the amount of undigested food that sits in the gut, causing discomfort.

Inflammation reducer: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort. By reducing inflammation, these compounds alleviate symptoms of bloating.

Muscle relaxant: Ginger's musculotropic effect helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, including the intestines and stomach, which can become tight and spasmodic during episodes of bloating. This relaxation helps to reduce cramping and discomfort associated with bloating.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: A sesquiterpene compound, zingiberene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate the discomfort associated with bloating.

Gingerols (6-Gingerol and 8-Gingerol): These phenolic compounds have anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce the severity of bloating.
Shogaols (6-Shogaol and 8-Shogaol): These vanilloid compounds have anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with bloating.
remedy_parts: Buds: Ginger buds are used in capsule form to provide relief from bloating due to their high content of gingerol, a compound that helps to ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of dried ginger root that has been aged for at least 6 months.
Grind the dried ginger root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ground ginger powder with 1/2 teaspoon of tapioca starch and 1/4 teaspoon of silica gel.
Use a capsule filler to fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the ginger powder mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with bloating because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the digestive tract. The curcumin present in turmeric also has natural antacid properties, neutralizing excess stomach acid and reducing gas production. As a result, herbal turmeric capsules can effectively alleviate symptoms of bloating, leaving you feeling lighter, more comfortable, and refreshed.

Turmerics Digestive Enzyme-Like Properties: Turmeric has been shown to increase digestion and absorption of nutrients, reducing the likelihood of undigested food particles contributing to bloating.

Antioxidant and Anti-Oxidative Stress Properties: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the digestive system from oxidative stress caused by poor diet or environmental toxins, which can lead to inflammation and bloating.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of bloating and discomfort in the digestive tract.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines that contribute to bloating and digestive discomfort.

Volatile oils (including turmerone and atlantone): Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are used due to their high content of curcumin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps alleviate bloating symptoms.
Roots: Curcuma longa roots are used for their rich content of curcuminoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, helping to alleviate bloating.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried curcuma longa roots in the early morning to ensure optimal quality.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 5g of the powder with 10g of gelatin in a small bowl and let it sit for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the curcuma longa mixture using a capsule filling machine and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with bloating because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work together to alleviate digestive discomfort. remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin capsules helps with bloating because they contain cumin oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system. Grind 20 grams of dried cuminum cyminum seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground cuminum cyminum powder with 50 grams of magnesium stearate and 20 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal them immediately.
Store the finished herbal cuminum cyminum capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two years.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and grind it into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground marshmallow root powder with 10 grams of powdered psyllium husk in a large bowl until well combined for 5 minutes.
Add 50 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir gently for 15 minutes until a smooth paste forms.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 250-300 milligrams of the herbal mixture using a capsule filler machine or spoon for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months before use for up to 2 years.

The herb's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpene lactones, work to stimulate digestion and relieve pressure on the digestive tract, which can help to alleviate bloating and discomfort associated with it. remedy_properties: Inulin: Dandelion root contains a type of fructan called inulin, which is not digested in the small intestine and instead feeds beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing bloating symptoms.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin found in dandelion root, has been shown to reduce inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
Anthraquinones: Dandelion root also contains anthraquinone glycosides, which have laxative properties that help stimulate bowel movements, reducing constipation and associated bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps regulate bowel movements and reduce bloating by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.
Buds: Dandelion buds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues and alleviate bloating symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from a trusted source or purchase them online.
Measure out 100 grams of the dried herb and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground herb with 20 grams of gelatin capsules and add 50 milliliters of water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill 500-count empty gelatin capsules with approximately 0.2 grams of the herb-gelatin mixture, sealing each capsule individually within 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year or refrigerate for longer shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with bloating because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. Carminative properties: Lavender's carminative effects can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and gas buildup that can contribute to bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with bloating.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and fiber, the seeds support healthy gut bacteria and digestion, alleviating symptoms of bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender flowers at a ratio of 50 grams to 1000 milliliters of menstruum.
Mix the harvested lavender flowers with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a 1:3 ratio.
Steep the mixture for 14 days under the light of the full moon for enhanced potency and effectiveness.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the herb residue and discard it.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the resulting lavender-infused oil and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with bloating because of its natural ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Seeds: Lemon balm seeds have been traditionally used to alleviate digestive discomfort, including bloating, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and stems in late summer or early fall weighing approximately 250 grams.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or air-drying for 3 days ensuring complete dryness.
Grind dried melissa officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to achieve 100% particle size consistency.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powdered melissa officinalis and seal immediately to maintain potency and freshness.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage maintaining quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with bloating because they contain a unique combination of antioxidants, amino acids, and polysaccharides that work synergistically to soothe and calm the digestive tract. Roots: Aloe roots have been traditionally used to treat stomach issues and indigestion, making them suitable for medicinal capsules that target bloating.
remedy_recipe: Measure one cup of dried aloe vera powder and mix it with half cup of dried peppermint leaves.
Add two teaspoons of ginger powder and one teaspoon of fennel seeds to the mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or manually.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for at least 24 hours before consumption.
Take two capsules three times daily with water after meals to help alleviate bloating symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with bloating because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive system. The capsules' active compound, cinnamaldehyde, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, alleviating symptoms of bloating such as abdominal discomfort and gas. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress, promoting a healthy gut microbiome that aids in digestion and reduces bloating. By taking herbal Ceylon cinnamon capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from bloating and enjoy a more comfortable digestive system.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon contains a high amount of antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of bloating such as cramps, pain, and discomfort.

Carminative properties: The essential oils present in Ceylon cinnamon, particularly cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to act as natural carminatives, which means they help relieve gas and bloating by reducing the formation of gas bubbles in the digestive system.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of bloating and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Grind 2 teaspoons of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground cinnamomum verum powder with 200ml of boiling water in a saucepan to create an infusion mixture for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix the infused liquid with 100g of gelatin and stir until fully dissolved, then fill empty capsules with the resulting solution.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with bloating because its active compounds, apigenin and luteolin, have a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the digestive system. remedy_recipe: Measure accurately 50 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in a clean glass container.
Combine the measured flower material with 500 ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Allow the mixture to steep for 20 minutes before filtering out the solids using a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Transfer the filtered liquid to a clean glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before adding 250 grams of gelatin capsules.
Fill each capsule with approximately 0.2 grams of the cooled herbal extract and seal the capsules in airtight containers for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for bloating are natural supplements that help alleviate uncomfortable and painful bloating symptoms. They work by soothing the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. The best herbal capsules for bloating include Foeniculum vulgare, Peumus boldus, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Cinchona officinalis, Iberis amara, Silybum marianum, Artemisia absinthium, and Angelica sinensis. If left untreated, bloating can cause significant discomfort, disrupt daily activities, and negatively impact relationships, work, and overall well-being, affecting one's quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bloating that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive disorders such as constipation, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal capsules containing ingredients like slippery elm, marshmallow root, and psyllium husk help alleviate symptoms of indigestion and discomfort. Additionally, herbs like peppermint, ginger, and turmeric are used to soothe stomach inflammation and reduce gas and bloating.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with bloating is a blend of Digestive Enzymes, Peppermint, and Ginger. Digestive Enzymes break down food in the digestive system, reducing gas and discomfort. Peppermint oil soothes the stomach and relaxes muscles, alleviating cramps and spasms. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that aid digestion and reduce bloating. This combination works synergistically to promote healthy digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate bloating symptoms. Taking these capsules after meals can provide relief and support overall digestive health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Explore (New York, N.Y.), herbal capsules containing a mix of two herbal medicines (Zataria-Trachyspermum, ZT) were found to be effective in reducing bloating in patients with Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS). The study involved 50 patients who were divided into three groups: a ZT capsule group, a placebo group, and a mebeverine capsule group. The results showed that after 4 weeks of treatment, symptoms of bloating, as well as pain and reflux, significantly decreased in the ZT group compared to the placebo and mebeverine groups (P < 0.05), indicating the potential benefits of herbal capsules in alleviating bloating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with bloating because they contain the essential oils found in fennel seeds, which have natural carminative properties. These properties help to ease digestion and relieve gas and bloating. The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel also soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's anise-like flavor helps to stimulate digestive enzymes, promoting healthy digestion and reducing symptoms of bloating. Regular use of fennel capsules may provide relief from bloating and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Helps with bloating by acting as an antispasmodic, reducing muscle contractions in the digestive tract that can contribute to discomfort and pain.
Anethole: Aids in alleviating bloating by exhibiting carminative properties, which help to expel gas from the digestive system and reduce abdominal discomfort.

Phenolic acids (e.g., caffeic acid, ferulic acid): Contribute to bloating relief by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used to make capsules for bloating due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate digestive discomfort.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are used to make capsules for bloating as they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of bloating and digestive issues.
Roots: Fennel roots are used to make capsules for bloating because they have carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm digestive issues and alleviate bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare in quantities of 100 grams to ensure sufficient material for capsule production.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the ground foeniculum vulgare powder with 1 part of a natural capsule filler like rice flour.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder-filler mixture using a capsule filling machine for 2 hours.
Label and date the capsules with information about the product and its usage instructions in 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peumus boldus capsules helps with bloating because they contain compounds that stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the gut. The herb has natural carminative properties, which help to release trapped gas and alleviate discomfort. By supporting a balanced gut microbiome, Peumus boldus capsules can provide relief from bloating and discomfort.

Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, which help soothe the digestive tract, reduce muscle spasms, and promote the relaxation of the digestive muscles, thereby alleviating bloating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants and are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate bloating symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the Peumus boldus tree contain flavonoids and tannins, which may help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with bloating.
Fruits: The fruits of the Peumus boldus tree are thought to have carminative properties, which help to release trapped gas in the digestive system and alleviate bloating.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of dried boldus leaves, ensuring the leaves are crushed into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 100g of the powdered boldus leaves with 300g of cornstarch, in a clean glass container, for 5 minutes.
Add 50g of glycerin and 50g of water to the mixture, stirring for 10 minutes to achieve uniform consistency.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 100 capsules, each containing 0.5g of the boldus mixture, for 30 minutes.
Store the herbal boldus capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with bloating because they contain the active compound curcumin, but more importantly, the bioactive compounds in ginger, particularly gingerols and shogaols, have natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. Shogaols: Shogaols have carminative properties, which help to relieve gas and alleviate bloating by promoting the release of trapped gas in the digestive system.

Gingerols and Shogaols' combination (also known as Ginger's anti-spasmodic effects): The combination of gingerols and shogaols has anti-spasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale are the most commonly used part for making capsules due to their high concentration of gingerols, which help alleviate bloating and digestive issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of zingiber officinale rhizomes from mature plants in early summer or late winter.
Clean and dry the rhizomes for 2 days in a well-ventilated area at 30-40°c.
Grind 50g of the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin and 20g of water in a bowl to create a smooth paste.
Fill 100 capsules with the paste using an automatic capsule filler in 5 minutes.

Mix the powder with 100 grams of gelatin in a bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried turmeric roots and sift them through a fine mesh sieve to remove impurities.
Grind the sifted turmeric roots into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or coffee grinder.
Mix 200 milliliters of water and 2 milliliters of glycerin with the ground turmeric powder to create a uniform mixture.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 100 gelatin capsules with the turmeric mixture and seal them tightly.

Grind the dried cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 30 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the fine powder with 1 gram of cellulose powder in a bowl within 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler machine within 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Iberis amara capsules helps with bloating because they contain the extract of Iberis amara, a plant traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues. Triterpenoids: Aid in bloating management by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive tract, promoting a healthy gut environment.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried iberis amara flowers and leaves  and 3 parts of carrier material by weight.
Weigh 250 milligrams of the iberis amara mixture and place it in a capsule mold.
Fill 100 capsule molds with the iberis amara mixture and seal them using a manual capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Check the capsules for moisture and quality after 2 weeks and discard any that are compromised.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum capsules helps with bloating because they contain the active compound silymarin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in flavonoids and silymarin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate bloating symptoms.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce bloating.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried silybum marianum fruits from a reputable source.
Mill the dried silybum marianum fruits into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 8 grams of excipient in a blending vessel.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the silybum marianum powder mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium capsules helps with bloating because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory properties of wormwood, which soothes the digestive system and reduces inflammation in the gut. remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone has a carminative effect, helping to relieve bloating and gas by reducing muscle contractions in the digestive tract.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate has a spasmolytic effect, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with bloating.
Bornyl alcohol: Bornyl alcohol has an anti-inflammatory effect, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their essential oils, which have carminative properties, helping to ease digestive discomfort and bloating.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers from a clean environment.
Crush the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 4 grams of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Add a small amount of water to the mixture and use a capsule filling machine to fill 50 capsules.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis capsules helps with bloating because they contain compounds that support digestive health and reduce inflammation. The root of the plant, also known as dong quai, has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to alleviate digestive issues, including bloating. This can lead to reduced water retention and a decrease in bloating symptoms, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and relaxed.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve bloating symptoms in the digestive system.
Butylphthalide: This sesquiterpene has a carminative effect, helping to eliminate gas and reduce bloating by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.

Angelan: This polysaccharide has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate bloating by promoting the health of the gut lining and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of ferulic acid, which helps alleviate bloating and digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: They contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, including volatile oils, that aid in reducing bloating and promoting digestive health.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of angelica sinensis roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of the fine powder with 400 grams of gelatin and 100 grams of glycerin in a large bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine for 30 minutes.
